io moth.d:
A large and handsome American moth (Hyperchiria Io), having
   a large, bright-colored spot on each hind wing, resembling the spots on
   the tail of a peacock. The larva is covered with prickly hairs, which
   sting like nettles.

iotacism.t: 希腊字母I(iota)或其发音的过分使用和重复
iotacism.d:
n. The frequent use of the sound of iota (that of English e
   in be), as among the modern Greeks; also, confusion from sounding /, /,
   /, /, //, etc., like /.
